title: 10 medicinal constituents of Picrasma excelsa  (sumatra)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Picrasma excelsa, also known as Sumatra quercetin, is a plant-based compound that exhibits impressive medicinal properties. Derived from the bark of the Picrasma excelsa tree, quercetin has been studied for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. It may also possess antimicrobial and antiviral properties, making it a promising natural remedy for various diseases and conditions.

Picrasma excelsa, also known as Sumatra, catechin is a type of flavonoid found in certain plants and trees. It has been shown to have numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect against chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer.

Picrasma excelsa, also known as Sumatra, kaempferol is a flavonoid compound extracted from the bark of Picrasma excelsa trees. This phytochemical has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Kaempferol has also been shown to have antiviral effects and may help protect against chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. Further research is needed to fully understand the benefits and mechanisms of action of Picrasma excelsa kaempferol.

Picrasma excelsa, also known as Sumatra, rutin is a type of bioflavonoid extracted from the bark and leaves of the Picrasma excelsa tree. It has been traditionally used in Asian medicine to treat various health issues, including cardiovascular disease, bleeding disorders, and inflammation. Rutin has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect against cell damage and improve overall health.

Picrasma excelsa, also known as Sumatra, gallic acid is a type of phenolic compound found in the bark of the Picrasma excelsa tree. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Gallic acid has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory effects and may have potential applications in treating various diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disorders. Its unique structure and bioactive properties make it an attractive compound for further research and development.

Picrasma excelsa, also known as Sumatra, flavonoids is a class of bioactive compounds isolated from the bark and leaves of this plant species. These flavonoids have been extensively studied for their potential therapeutic properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. They have been shown to exhibit strong inhibitory effects on various enzymes and may have applications in the prevention and treatment of various diseases, such as cardiovascular disease and cancer.

Picrasma excelsa, also known as Sumatra, quinic acid is a bioactive compound extracted from its bark. This natural product has been studied for its potential pharmacological properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Quinic acid has been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, making it a promising candidate for the treatment of inflammatory diseases, such as arthritis and asthma. Its antioxidant activity has also been shown to protect against oxidative stress-induced cell damage.

Picrasma excelsa, also known as Sumatra, caffeic acid is a type of phenolic compound found in the bark and leaves of the Picrasma excelsa tree. It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. Caffeic acid has also been shown to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions. Further research is needed to fully explore its medicinal benefits.

Picrasma excelsa, also known as Sumatra, chlorogenic acid is a polyphenolic compound extracted from the bark of the Picrasma excelsa tree. This natural compound has been extensively studied for its potential medicinal properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Chlorogenic acid has been found to exhibit significant inhibitory effects on certain enzymes and may have therapeutic applications in the treatment of various diseases, such as hypertension and cancer.

Picrasma excelsa, also known as Sumatra, ellagic acid is a bioactive compound extracted from the bark of Picrasma excelsa trees. This potent antioxidant has been studied for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Ellagic acid has also shown promise in inhibiting cancer cell growth and improving cardiovascular health. Further research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health, but its unique chemical structure makes it a promising area of study.

intro: Picrasma excelsa has active constituents such as flavonoids, terpenes, and alkaloids. These bioactive compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine. The anti-inflammatory effects can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory diseases, while the antioxidant properties may protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease.
intro_desc: Picrasma excelsa has active constituents such as flavonoids, triterpenes, and phenolic acids. These constituents possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to the plant's medicinal value. For instance, its flavonoids can help alleviate fever and reduce inflammation, while its phenolic acids can act as natural antioxidants to protect against oxidative stress. These properties can improve lives by providing relief from various health conditions, such as fever, inflammation, and oxidative damage, ultimately enhancing overall well-being.
